title: Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for Real Estate
metaDescription: Done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up for real estate. Every offer, listing presentation, and management quote gets chased on time, in your voice.
excerpt: A done-for-you follow-up capability for real estate offices. Every proposal, listing presentation, and management quote gets chased on schedule, in your voice.

sections.intro:
In real estate, deals do not die from bad pricing. They die from silence. A listing presentation goes out on Tuesday, the seller says they want to think, and by Friday a competing agent has already followed up twice. A management quote sits in a landlord's inbox while another agency calls them. You know the work. You just do not have the hours to chase every proposal on the day it needs chasing.

The Ayothedoc AI Operating System (AIOS) installs a proposal and quote follow-up capability that runs on a schedule, in your voice, using your CRM and your inbox. It knows which proposals are open, what stage each one is at, when the last touch happened, and what to send next. You stay in control. The system does the chasing.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Capabilities layer of the AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, summarize, follow up, and report. To work well, this capability leans on the other three layers:

  • Context: your offers, your fee structures, your voice, and how you talk to vendors, buyers, and landlords.
  • Connections: your CRM, inbox, calendar, and document or e-signature tool, so the system works from live data instead of guesses.
  • Cadence: the schedule that fires the follow-ups at day 1, day 3, day 7, and beyond, without anyone remembering to press a button.

If you have not seen it yet, the free 60-Second Lead Engine handles the front of the funnel. This capability takes over once a proposal or quote has gone out.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed, here is what you get:

  • A single live view of every open proposal, listing presentation, and management quote, with stage, age, and next action.
  • Drafted follow-up emails in your voice, sent on a schedule you approve, with the listing or quote referenced specifically.
  • First follow-up within 24 hours of the proposal going out. Second touch on day 3. Third on day 7. A polite close-out on day 14 if there is no reply.
  • Replies routed to the right agent with a short summary and a suggested next step.
  • A weekly digest showing which proposals are warm, which went cold, and which need a human call today.
  • An audit trail in the CRM so nothing falls between agents or assistants.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install the system in three phases. You stay involved only where it matters.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at how proposals and quotes currently leave your office, where follow-up breaks down, and which deals you suspect were lost to silence. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we connect your CRM, inbox, and document tool, load your voice and templates, set the follow-up cadence, and test against real recent proposals before going live.
  3. Operate (ongoing): the system runs on schedule. We add one new automation per week, tune the voice, and adjust the cadence based on reply data. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Follow-up coverage on open proposals moves from roughly 30 to 50 percent to 100 percent, because the system does not forget.
  • Reply rates on listing presentations and management quotes typically rise by 15 to 35 percent within the first 60 days.
  • Agents recover 5 to 10 hours a week that used to go to chasing, recapping, and rewriting the same email.
  • Across the AIOS, the standing guarantee applies: you recover 40 or more hours a month or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the follow-ups sound like a robot wrote them?

No. During install we load your past proposals, your tone, and the phrases you actually use with vendors and landlords. Drafts go through a short approval window at first, then run on schedule once you trust the voice.

What happens when a vendor or landlord replies?

The reply is routed to the right agent with a one-paragraph summary and a suggested next step. The system stops automated follow-ups on that proposal the moment a real conversation starts.

Do we have to change CRM or e-signature tools?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and sits on top of what you already use. If your current stack has a gap, we will say so plainly during the audit, but switching tools is never a precondition.
